Aula 16 -- Pre-class

Asking About the City -- A Guided Tour & Getting Information

A guided tour of Paris: tour vocabulary (tour, sightseeing, landmark, river cruise, brochure) and the language to ask for information politely ("Can you tell me...?", "Is there...?"). New vocabulary: tour, sightseeing, landmark, river cruise, view, brochure, opening hours, souvenir, nearby.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Tour -- a visit to many interesting places, often with a guide
"The city tour starts at ten o'clock." (Passeio / Tour)
Sightseeing -- the activity of visiting famous and interesting places
"We went sightseeing all morning in Paris." (Passear pelos pontos turísticos)
Landmark -- a famous building or place that is easy to recognize
"The Eiffel Tower is the most famous landmark in Paris." (Ponto de referência)
River cruise -- a trip on a boat along a river to see the city
"The river cruise gives you a great view of Paris." (Passeio de barco no rio)
View -- everything you can see from a place, often beautiful
"From the boat, the view of the city is wonderful." (Vista)
Brochure -- a small paper book with pictures and information about a place
"Take a brochure; it has a map of the tour." (Folheto)
Opening hours -- the times when a place is open for visitors
"The opening hours are from nine to six." (Horário de funcionamento)
Souvenir -- a small thing you buy to remember a place or trip
"I bought a souvenir for my mom at the shop." (Lembrança / Souvenir)
Nearby -- close to here, only a short distance away
"There is an old church nearby." (Por perto / Próximo)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Tour
Sightseeing
Landmark
River cruise
View
Brochure
Opening hours
Souvenir
Nearby

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela goes on a guided tour in Paris. At the start, she asks, "Can you tell me where the tour starts?" The guide shows her the first landmark. Then the group takes a river cruise, and the view from the boat is wonderful. Gabriela asks, "Is there a place to take photos?" The guide says yes. After the cruise, they go sightseeing and visit an old church nearby. She asks about the opening hours, and they are from nine to six. At the end, she takes a brochure with a map and buys a small souvenir for her mom.

1. How does Gabriela ask politely where the tour starts?
A "Can you tell me where the tour starts?"
B "Where starts the tour you?"
C "How much is the tour?"
2. What gives a wonderful view of the city?
A The brochure.
B The river cruise from the boat.
C The souvenir shop.
3. What are the opening hours of the church?
A From ten to two.
B From nine to six.
C All night.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Can you tell me...? & Is there...?

GRAMMAR

Usamos "Can you tell me...?" para pedir informação de forma educada e "Is there...?" para perguntar se algo existe. Depois de "Can you tell me", mantemos a ordem normal (sujeito + verbo). / We use "Can you tell me...?" to ask for information politely and "Is there...?" to ask if something exists.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Ask politely (place)Can you tell me where + subject + verb?Can you tell me where the tour starts?
Ask politely (time)Can you tell me what time + subject + verb?Can you tell me what time it opens?
Ask politely (length)Can you tell me how long + subject + verb?Can you tell me how long the tour is?
Ask if it existsIs there + a/an + noun?Is there a shop nearby?
Short answerYes, there is. / No, there isn't.Yes, there is.

Key point / Ponto-chave: depois de Can you tell me, mantenha a ordem normal (sujeito + verbo): "Can you tell me where the tour starts?" (não "where does the tour start"). Para perguntar se algo existe, use Is there + a/an + substantivo.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"Can you tell me the tour starts?"
"Can you tell me it opens?"
" a souvenir shop nearby?"
"The Eiffel Tower is a famous ."
"Take a ; it has a map."
"I bought a for my mom."

Stage 2: Put the Tour Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de um passeio guiado na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, you take a river cruise and you enjoy the view of the city.
?First, you meet the guide and you ask, can you tell me where the tour starts?
?Finally, you buy a souvenir and you take a brochure with a map.
?Next, you walk to the first landmark and you listen to the guide.
?Then, you visit an old church nearby and you ask about the opening hours.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

Can you tell me where the tour starts?
Você pode me dizer onde o passeio começa?
Is there a souvenir shop nearby?
Tem uma loja de lembranças por perto?
Can you tell me how long the tour is?
Você pode me dizer quanto tempo dura o passeio?
The view from the river cruise is wonderful.
A vista do passeio de barco é maravilhosa.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to know politely where the tour starts. You ask:
A "Can you tell me where the tour starts?"
B "Can you tell me where does the tour start?"
C "Where the tour?"
You want to know if there is a shop nearby. You ask:
A "There is a shop nearby?"
B "Is there a shop nearby?"
C "Is nearby a shop there?"
You want to know what time the cruise leaves. You ask:
A "Can you tell me what time leaves the cruise?"
B "Can you tell me what time the cruise leaves?"
C "What time the cruise?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine you are on a guided tour in Paris. Out loud, ask the guide for information: where the tour starts, what time the cruise leaves, how long it is, and if there is a souvenir shop nearby. Use "Can you tell me..." and "Is there..." Use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 16

1Can you tell me where the tour starts?Você pode me dizer onde o passeio começa?
2Can you tell me what time it opens?Você pode me dizer a que horas abre?
3Is there a souvenir shop nearby?Tem uma loja de lembranças por perto?
4Can you tell me how long the tour is?Você pode me dizer quanto tempo dura o passeio?
5The view from the river cruise is wonderful.A vista do passeio de barco é maravilhosa.
Aula 17 -- Pre-class

Buying Gifts & Making Choices

Shopping for gifts in a Paris gift shop: gift vocabulary (gift, present, postcard, keychain, jewelry) and the language to choose between things ("Which one...?", "I'll take the blue one", "I prefer the small ones"). New vocabulary: gift, gift shop, present, postcard, keychain, magnet, jewelry, wallet, wrapping paper.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Gift -- a thing you give to someone, often for a special day
"I bought a gift for my best friend." (Presente)
Gift shop -- a store where you buy presents and souvenirs
"There is a gift shop at the end of the tour." (Loja de presentes)
Present -- another word for a gift: a thing you give to someone
"This scarf is a present for my mom." (Presente)
Postcard -- a small card with a picture that you send by mail
"I sent a postcard of the Eiffel Tower to my friend." (Cartão-postal)
Keychain -- a small ring or chain that you put your keys on
"The blue keychain is very popular." (Chaveiro)
Magnet -- a small object that sticks to metal, often on a fridge
"I bought a fridge magnet for my dad." (Ímã / ímã de geladeira)
Jewelry -- pretty things you wear, like rings, necklaces and earrings
"The silver jewelry is perfect for a present." (Jóias / bijuterias)
Wallet -- a small flat case where you keep your money and cards
"I bought a leather wallet for my brother." (Carteira)
Wrapping paper -- colorful paper you use to cover a present
"The clerk wrapped my gift in nice wrapping paper." (Papel de presente)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Gift
Gift shop
Present
Postcard
Keychain
Magnet
Jewelry
Wallet
Wrapping paper

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela goes to a gift shop in Paris to buy a present for everyone. The clerk asks, "Which one would you like?" Gabriela looks at the postcards. There are big ones and small ones, and she takes three small ones for her friends. For the keychain, she says, "I'll take the blue one." For her mom, the silver jewelry is perfect. She also buys a magnet for her dad and a wallet for her brother. The clerk covers each gift in nice wrapping paper.

1. How does the clerk ask Gabriela to choose?
A "Which one would you like?"
B "How much is the tour?"
C "Where are you from?"
2. Which postcards does she take?
A The big ones.
B Three small ones.
C None of them.
3. What does she choose for her mom?
A A blue keychain.
B The silver jewelry.
C A leather wallet.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- one / ones & Which one

GRAMMAR

Para não repetir o substantivo, usamos "one" para uma coisa e "ones" para várias. Perguntamos "Which one...?" e escolhemos com "I'll take...". / To avoid repeating the noun, we use "one" for one thing and "ones" for many.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Ask which (choose)Which one + question?Which one would you like?
One thing (singular)the + adjective + onethe blue one
Many things (plural)the + adjective + onesthe small ones
Buy itI'll take + the one/onesI'll take the silver ones.
PreferI prefer + the one/onesI prefer the small ones.

Key point / Ponto-chave: use one para uma coisa e ones para várias, sem repetir o substantivo: "the blue one" (não "the blue keychain one"). Pergunte com Which one e compre com I'll take.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"I'll take the blue , please."
"I prefer the small ."
"Which would you like?"
"This scarf is a for my mom."
"The silver is perfect for a present."
"Can you put it in ?"

Stage 2: Put the Gift Shop Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de comprar um presente na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, the clerk asks, which one would you like?
?First, you walk into the gift shop and you look at the gifts.
?Finally, the clerk wraps your present in wrapping paper and you pay.
?Next, you find the postcards and you choose the small ones.
?Then, you say, I'll take the blue one, please.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

I'll take the blue one, please.
Vou levar o azul, por favor.
I prefer the small ones.
Eu prefiro os pequenos.
Which one do you recommend?
Qual você recomenda?
Can you put it in wrapping paper?
Você pode embrulhar para presente?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want one blue keychain. You say:
A "I'll take the blue one."
B "I'll take the blue ones."
C "I'll take the blue one keychain."
You prefer the small postcards (many). You say:
A "I prefer the small one."
B "I prefer the small ones."
C "I prefer the small one postcards."
You want the clerk to help you choose one magnet. You ask:
A "Which ones do you recommend?"
B "Which one do you recommend?"
C "Which magnet one?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine you are in a gift shop in Paris. Out loud, choose presents for three people: answer "Which one would you like?", say "I'll take the blue one" and "I prefer the small ones", and ask for wrapping paper. Use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 17

1I'll take the blue one, please.Vou levar o azul, por favor.
2I prefer the small ones.Eu prefiro os pequenos.
3Which one do you recommend?Qual você recomenda?
4This is a present for my mom.Este é um presente para a minha mãe.
5Can you put it in wrapping paper?Você pode embrulhar para presente?
Aula 18 -- Pre-class

Sending a Postcard

At the post office in Paris: mail vocabulary (stamp, envelope, mailbox, address, parcel) and the short words to say who you send things to ("send it to her", "send them to him"). New vocabulary: post office, stamp, envelope, address, mailbox, abroad, by air mail, parcel, weigh.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Post office -- a place where you send letters, postcards and parcels
"I went to the post office to send my postcards." (Correio / agência dos correios)
Stamp -- a small paper you stick on mail to pay for sending it
"The stamp for Brazil is one euro eighty." (Selo)
Envelope -- a flat paper cover that you put a letter inside
"I put the letter in an envelope and wrote the address." (Envelope)
Address -- the place where someone lives, that you write on the mail
"I copied my grandmother's address on the postcard." (Endereço)
Mailbox -- a box on the street where you put letters to send them
"I put the postcard in the yellow mailbox." (Caixa de correio)
Abroad -- in or to another country, far from home
"To send a postcard abroad, you need a bigger stamp." (Para o exterior)
By air mail -- when a letter or postcard travels to another country by plane
"I sent it to Brazil by air mail." (Por via aérea)
Parcel -- a box or package that you send to someone by mail
"The clerk weighed a small parcel for my mom." (Encomenda / pacote)
Weigh -- to measure how heavy something is, often on a scale
"The clerk will weigh your parcel to find the price." (Pesar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Post office
Stamp
Envelope
Address
Mailbox
Abroad
By air mail
Parcel
Weigh

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela goes to the post office in Paris. She has a postcard for her mom, and she wants to send it to her in Brazil. The clerk says, "That is abroad, so you need a stamp for one euro eighty, and we send it by air mail." Gabriela also has two postcards for her friends, so she sends them too. She wrote the address on each one. For her dad, she has a small parcel; the clerk will weigh it and send it to him. Then Gabriela puts everything in the yellow mailbox.

1. Where does Gabriela want to send the postcard for her mom?
A To her in Brazil.
B To the museum.
C To the gift shop.
2. What does she do with the two postcards for her friends?
A She keeps them.
B She sends them too.
C She gives them to the clerk for free.
3. What does the clerk do with the parcel for her dad?
A He opens it.
B He weighs it and sends it to him.
C He throws it away.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- it / them / her / him

GRAMMAR

Para não repetir o nome, usamos uma palavra curta depois do verbo: "it" para uma coisa, "them" para várias, "her" para uma mulher, "him" para um homem. / To avoid repeating the name, we use a short word after the verb.

Instead of / Em vez deWe say / DizemosExample / Exemplo
the postcard (uma coisa)itI'll send it.
two postcards (várias coisas)themI'll send them.
my mom (mulher)hersend it to her.
my dad (homem)himsend it to him.
my friends (pessoas)themwrite to them.

Key point / Ponto-chave: depois do verbo, use a palavra curta para não repetir o nome: it (uma coisa), them (várias coisas ou pessoas), her (mulher), him (homem). Estrutura: send + it/them + to + her/him/them.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"I have a postcard. I'll send to my friend."
"I have two postcards. I'll send to Brazil."
"This is for my mom. I'll send it to ."
"This is for my dad. I'll send it to ."
"I put a on the postcard."
"I sent it abroad ."

Stage 2: Put the Postcard Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de enviar um postal na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, you go to the post office and you choose a stamp.
?First, you buy a postcard and you write a short message on the back.
?Finally, you put it in the yellow mailbox and you send it to them.
?Next, you copy the address of your family carefully.
?Then, you give the postcard to the clerk and you pay.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

I want to send it to her in Brazil.
Quero enviar isto para ela no Brasil.
Can I send them too?
Posso enviar estes também?
How much is a stamp for Brazil?
Quanto custa um selo para o Brasil?
Can you weigh this parcel?
Você pode pesar esta encomenda?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You have one postcard for your mom. You say:
A "I'll send it to her."
B "I'll send them to her."
C "I'll send it to him."
You have three postcards for your friends. You say:
A "I'll send it to them."
B "I'll send them to my friends."
C "I'll send them to her."
You want to send a postcard to Brazil. You ask:
A "Where is the museum?"
B "How much is a stamp to send it abroad?"
C "Which one would you like?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine you are at the post office in Paris. Out loud, send a postcard to your mom, two postcards to your friends, and a parcel to your dad. Use "it", "them", "her" and "him", and use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 18

1I want to send it to her in Brazil.Quero enviar isto para ela no Brasil.
2Can I send them too?Posso enviar estes também?
3How much is a stamp for Brazil?Quanto custa um selo para o Brasil?
4Can you weigh this parcel?Você pode pesar esta encomenda?
5Where is the mailbox?Onde fica a caixa de correio?
Aula 19 -- Pre-class

Making Plans with Friends

Arranging to meet up with friends in Paris: plan vocabulary (plan, meet up, get together, hang out, free, busy) and the language to talk about future plans ("I'm meeting Lucas at four", "We're going to the movies") and to suggest ("Let's meet up", "Why don't we get together?"). New vocabulary: plan, meet up, get together, hang out, free, busy, on time, downtown, looking forward to.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

Plan -- an idea about what you will do, where and when
"My plan for Saturday is to see my friends." (Plano)
Meet up -- to see your friends at a place you both choose
"Let's meet up at the cafe at four." (Encontrar-se)
Get together -- when a group of friends meet to spend time together
"We get together every Saturday." (Reunir-se / juntar-se)
Hang out -- to spend relaxed, easy time with friends
"We're going to hang out at the park." (Passar o tempo / curtir)
Free -- available, with nothing you must do at that time
"Are you free on Saturday afternoon?" (Livre / disponível)
Busy -- with a lot of things to do, so not available
"I'm busy in the morning with my class." (Ocupado/a)
On time -- at the right time, not early and not late
"Please be on time; the movie starts at six." (Na hora / pontual)
Downtown -- the central, busy part of a city, with shops and cafes
"We're meeting downtown near the park." (Centro da cidade)
Looking forward to -- feeling happy and excited about something that will happen
"I'm looking forward to the weekend." (Ansioso/a por / mal posso esperar)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

Plan
Meet up
Get together
Hang out
Free
Busy
On time
Downtown
Looking forward to

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela has a plan for Saturday. Her friend Lucas calls and says, "We're getting together this weekend. Are you free?" In the morning she's busy with her French class, but in the afternoon she's free. Lucas says, "Let's meet up at the cafe downtown at four. Then we're going to the movies at six." Gabriela wants to be on time, so she writes the time down. "Why don't we hang out at the cafe first?" Lucas asks. "Perfect," says Gabriela. "I'm really looking forward to it!"

1. When is Gabriela free?
A In the morning.
B In the afternoon.
C She is busy all day.
2. Where are they meeting up?
A At the cafe downtown.
B At the post office.
C At her French class.
3. What are they doing after the cafe?
A Going home.
B Going to the movies at six.
C Going to the French class.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Continuous for the Future & Let's / Why don't we

GRAMMAR

Para um plano futuro já combinado, usamos be (am/is/are) + verbo-ing, geralmente com um horário ou dia. Para sugerir, usamos "Let's + verbo" ou "Why don't we + verbo?". / For a future plan you arranged, use be + verb-ing. To suggest, use Let's or Why don't we.

Form / FormaStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Plan (I)I am + verb-ingI'm meeting Lucas at four.
Plan (we / they)We are + verb-ingWe're going to the movies.
Plan (he / she)He/She is + verb-ingLucas is coming too.
Suggest (idea)Let's + simple verbLet's meet up at the cafe.
Suggest (question)Why don't we + verb?Why don't we get together?

Key point / Ponto-chave: para um plano futuro use be + verbo-ing ("I'm meeting", não "I meet"). Depois de Let's, use o verbo simples ("Let's go", não "Let's to go").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"I'm Lucas at four o'clock."
" meet up at the cafe."
"Are you on Saturday?"
"In the morning I'm with my class."
"Please be ; the movie starts at six."
"I'm really it."

Stage 2: Put the Plan Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de combinar um plano na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, your friend asks, where are we getting together?
?First, you send a message to your friend.
?Finally, you confirm the plan and you say, see you on Saturday.
?Next, you suggest a time and you say, let's meet at four.
?Then, you agree on a place and you say, we're meeting at the cafe downtown.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

I'm meeting Lucas at four o'clock.
Vou encontrar o Lucas às quatro horas.
Let's meet up at the cafe.
Vamos nos encontrar no café.
Are you free on Saturday?
Você está livre no sábado?
Why don't we get together downtown?
Por que não nos reunimos no centro?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You have a plan to see Lucas at four. You say:
A "I'm meeting Lucas at four."
B "I meet Lucas at four."
C "I meeting Lucas at four."
You want to suggest going to the cafe. You say:
A "Let's to go to the cafe."
B "Let's go to the cafe."
C "Let's going to the cafe."
A friend asks if you can come Saturday morning, but you have your class. You say:
A "I'm free in the morning."
B "I'm busy in the morning, but free in the afternoon."
C "I am on time in the morning."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Imagine you are making a plan with a friend for Saturday. Out loud, invite your friend, suggest a time and a place with "Let's" or "Why don't we", say what you are doing with "I'm meeting..." or "We're going...", and remind them to be on time. Use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 19

1I'm meeting Lucas at four o'clock.Vou encontrar o Lucas às quatro horas.
2Let's meet up at the cafe.Vamos nos encontrar no café.
3Are you free on Saturday?Você está livre no sábado?
4Why don't we get together downtown?Por que não nos reunimos no centro?
5I'm looking forward to it. Please be on time.Mal posso esperar. Por favor, chegue na hora.
Aula 20 -- Pre-class

My Paris Trip So Far

Looking back at the whole trip: trip vocabulary (highlight, memory, experience, unforgettable) and the language to talk about your experiences with the present perfect ("I have already visited the museum", "I have not seen the tower yet", "Have you ever tried a croissant?"). New vocabulary: so far, amazing, unforgettable, highlight, memory, experience, miss, capture, journal.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para ouvir. / Listen to each word and read the example.

So far -- from the beginning until now
"My trip has been amazing so far." (Até agora)
Amazing -- very good, wonderful, that surprises you in a good way
"Paris is an amazing city." (Incrível)
Unforgettable -- so special that you remember it forever
"This is an unforgettable trip." (Inesquecível)
Highlight -- the best or most special part of an event
"The highlight of my trip was the museum." (Ponto alto)
Memory -- a thing you remember from the past
"I have a beautiful memory of Paris." (Lembrança / memória)
Experience -- a thing you do or that happens to you and you learn from
"Travel is an amazing experience." (Experiência)
Miss -- to feel sad because a person or place is not with you
"I miss my family a little." (Sentir falta / sentir saudade)
Capture -- to keep a moment forever, for example in a photo
"I want to capture every memory." (Capturar / registrar)
Journal -- a book where you write about your days and feelings
"I write about my trip in my journal." (Diário)

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Ligue cada palavra à sua tradução. / Match each word with its translation.

So far
Amazing
Unforgettable
Highlight
Memory
Experience
Miss
Capture
Journal

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas. / Read the text and answer the questions.

Gabriela's trip to Paris has been amazing so far. It is an unforgettable experience. She has already visited a big museum and she has already seen the Eiffel Tower. She has just written three postcards to her friends. The highlight of her trip was the gift shop. She wants to capture every memory in her journal. She has not gone to the top of the tower yet, but she will go tomorrow. She misses her family a little, but this is the best trip of her life.

1. What has Gabriela already done?
A She has visited a museum and seen the tower.
B She has gone home.
C She has not done anything.
2. What has she NOT done yet?
A Written postcards.
B Gone to the top of the tower.
C Visited a museum.
3. What was the highlight of her trip?
A The airport.
B The gift shop.
C The hotel.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect (already / yet / just / ever)

GRAMMAR

Para falar de experiências até agora, usamos "have / has" + o verbo na 3a forma (visited, seen, written, been). / To talk about experiences until now, we use "have / has" + the past participle.

Use / UsoStructure / EstruturaExample / Exemplo
Done / Feito (already)have + already + verbI have already visited the museum.
Not done / Não feito (yet)have not + verb + yetI have not seen the tower yet.
A moment ago / Há pouco (just)have + just + verbI have just written a postcard.
Ask / Perguntar (ever)Have + you + ever + verb?Have you ever tried a croissant?
3rd form / 3a formavisit→visited, see→seen, go→beenwritten, done, been, seen

Key point / Ponto-chave: use have + verb (3a forma) para experiências até agora. already = já feito, yet = ainda não, just = há pouco, ever = já, na sua vida (em perguntas). Diga "I have visited" (não "I have visit").

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra certa. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase. / Complete each sentence with the correct word.

"I have visited the museum."
"I have not seen the tower ."
"Have you traveled abroad?"
"The of my trip was the museum."
"I want to capture every ."
"This is an unforgettable ."

Stage 2: Put the Trip Recap Steps in Order

Order

Ouça primeiro, depois coloque os passos de contar a sua viagem na ordem certa. / Listen first, then put the steps in the correct order.

?After that, you say what you have not done yet, like, I have not seen the tower yet.
?First, you think about the highlights of your trip.
?Finally, you share your favorite memory of the trip.
?Next, you say what you have already done, like, I have already visited the museum.
?Then, you ask a friend, have you ever traveled abroad?

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e grave você mesma dizendo. Fale devagar e com clareza. / Listen, then record yourself.

I have already visited the museum.
Eu já visitei o museu.
I have not seen the tower yet.
Eu ainda não vi a torre.
Have you ever traveled abroad?
Você já viajou para o exterior?
I want to capture every memory in my journal.
Eu quero registrar cada lembrança no meu diário.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor frase para cada situação. / Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You visited the museum this morning. You say:
A "I have already visited the museum."
B "I have already visit the museum."
C "I have museum visited already."
You have not gone to the tower. You say:
A "I have not went to the tower yet."
B "I have not gone to the tower yet."
C "I have not go to the tower already."
You want to ask a friend about a life experience. You ask:
A "Did you ever traveled abroad?"
B "Have you ever traveled abroad?"
C "Have you ever travel abroad yet?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesma respondendo à pergunta. Não há resposta certa ou errada. / Record yourself answering.

Tell the story of your Paris trip so far. Out loud, say two things you have already done, one thing you have not done yet, the highlight of your trip, and your favorite memory. Use at least four of today's words.

Survival Card -- Lesson 20

1I have already visited the museum.Eu já visitei o museu.
2I have not seen the tower yet.Eu ainda não vi a torre.
3Have you ever traveled abroad?Você já viajou para o exterior?
4The highlight was an unforgettable experience.O ponto alto foi uma experiência inesquecível.
5I want to capture every memory in my journal.Eu quero registrar cada lembrança no meu diário.
Aula 21 — Pre-class

Will You or Won't You? — Future with 'Will' for Predictions, Promises & Spontaneous Decisions

Depois desta aula, você projeta seu futuro em inglês — o que VAI acontecer em 2027 e o que você promete fazer.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de fazer 5+ predições e 3+ promessas em inglês sobre 2027 usando will, won't, probably, maybe, I hope e I promise — sem tradução do PT.

Vocabulário Vocabulário

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

will·vai (futuro/promessa/decisão)
“I will study English every day.”
won't·não vai (futuro negativo)
“I won't forget my passport.”
probably·provavelmente
“It will probably rain in Paris.”
maybe·talvez
“Maybe I will live in France one day.”
definitely·com certeza
“I will definitely visit the Louvre.”
I think·eu acho (que)
“I think it will be amazing.”
I hope·eu espero
“I hope I will be fluent by 2027.”
I promise·eu prometo
“I promise I will practice every day.”
exchange program·programa de intercâmbio
“Maybe I will join an exchange program.”
gap year·ano sabático
“I might take a gap year after high school.”

V1. Match the words to their meanings Vocabulary

Selecione a tradução correta para cada palavra. Errou? Leia o exemplo de novo — é aí que se aprende.

will
won't
probably
maybe
definitely
I hope
I promise
exchange program

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas sobre a gramática.

GABRIELA'S PLANS FOR 2027

Next year, I will travel to Paris with my family. I think it will probably rain in December, but I won't care because I love the city. I promise I will study English every day before the trip. I definitely won't forget my passport! Maybe I will visit the Eiffel Tower on the first day. I hope I will understand French people when they speak English. One thing is certain: I won't be afraid to try!

1. What does Gabriela use "will" for in "I will travel to Paris"?
A A routine she does every day.
B A prediction or plan about the future.
C Something that happened yesterday.
2. "I promise I will study every day" — what does "will" express here?
A A prediction.
B A promise.
C A question.
3. What is the negative form of "will"?
A willn't
B don't will
C won't

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip GRAMMAR

FUTURE WITH WILL — PREDICTIONS, PROMISES & DECISIONS

Usamos will para falar sobre o futuro: previsões, promessas e decisões espontâneas. A forma negativa é won't (will not).

UsoAfirmativoNegativoTradução
PrevisãoIt will rain.It won't rain.Vai chover. / Não vai chover.
PromessaI will study every day.I won't forget.Eu vou estudar. / Eu não vou esquecer.
DecisãoI will visit the tower.I won't be afraid.Eu vou visitar. / Eu não vou ter medo.

Pergunta: Will you travel? — Yes, I will. / No, I won't.

Modificadores: I will probably go. / Maybe I will go. / I definitely won't go.

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Lê a dica em inglês com calma — é assim que se aprende de verdade.

"I definitely visit the Louvre in Paris."
Hint: the modal that expresses a future decision or promise
"I forget my passport — I promise!"
Hint: the contraction of "will not"
"It will rain in Paris in February."
Hint: an adverb that means "more likely than not"
" I will live in France one day."
Hint: a word that expresses uncertainty about the future
"I I will be fluent by my trip in 2027."
Hint: to wish for something positive in the future
"I I will study English every single day."
Hint: to give your word that you will do something

P2. Choose the best answer Quiz

Clica na resposta. Verde = certo, vermelho = tente de novo. Errou? Lê tudo de novo com calma.

You see dark clouds in Paris and your friend asks if it will rain. The most natural answer is:
A "It rains."
B "Yes, it raining."
C "Yes, it will probably rain."
D "It rained yesterday."
You want to make a strong promise about studying English. The best sentence is:
A "I am studying every day."
B "I promise I will study every day."
C "I would study every day."
D "I studied every day."

P3. Put this 2027 prediction speech in order Practice

Toque em cada item na ordem certa, ou use as setas para mover. Quando estiver pronta, clique em Check Order.

?"I will visit the Louvre and the Eiffel Tower for sure."
?"I think it will probably be cold and a little rainy."
?"I won't speak Portuguese — only English and a little French!"
?"In February 2027, I will fly to Paris with my family."
?"I promise I will practice every day until then."

P4. Read aloud Speaking

Ouça a frase, depois grave você falando. Use o Chrome — o sistema compara palavra por palavra.

I will definitely visit Paris in February 2027.
I won't forget my passport — I promise!
I think it will probably rain, but I won't mind.

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

Imagine it is December 2026 — Paris is two months away. Speak directly to your future self in English: what WILL you do every day until February? What WON'T you do? What do you HOPE will happen in Paris? Use will, won't, probably, maybe, I hope, I promise.
Sugestão de resposta
“Future Gabriela, I will study English every day. I won't skip class. I will probably watch one episode of Friends every night in English. I hope I will be fluent enough to make a French friend. I promise I will not be afraid to try.”

Survival Card — Lesson 21: Will You or Won't You?

1I will definitely visit the Louvre.Eu vou com certeza visitar o Louvre.
2I won't forget my passport.Eu não vou esquecer meu passaporte.
3I think it will probably rain in Paris.Eu acho que provavelmente vai chover em Paris.
4I hope I will be fluent by 2027.Eu espero estar fluente até 2027.
5I promise I will practice every day.Eu prometo que vou praticar todo dia.
Aula 22 — Pre-class

At the Airport — Check-In, Security, Gates & Surviving the First International Flight

Depois desta aula, você atravessa um aeroporto inteiro em Paris sem travar — check-in, segurança, embarque.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de atravessar um cenário completo de aeroporto (check-in, segurança, portão) em inglês — usando May I, Could you, e o vocabulário operacional de viagem.

Vocabulário Vocabulário

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

passport·passaporte
“My passport is in my carry-on bag.”
boarding pass·cartão de embarque
“Here is my boarding pass.”
check-in counter·balcão de check-in
“The check-in counter is over there.”
carry-on luggage·bagagem de mão
“This is my carry-on luggage.”
gate·portão de embarque
“My flight leaves from gate B12.”
security checkpoint·controle de segurança
“The security checkpoint is on the second floor.”
flight attendant·comissário de bordo
“Could you ask the flight attendant?”
delay·atraso
“My flight has a one-hour delay.”
window seat·assento na janela
“I prefer a window seat.”
aisle seat·assento no corredor
“I would like an aisle seat.”

V1. Match the words to their meanings Vocabulary

Selecione a tradução correta para cada palavra. Errou? Leia o exemplo de novo — é aí que se aprende.

passport
boarding pass
gate
security checkpoint
flight attendant
delay
window seat
carry-on luggage

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas sobre a gramática.

GABRIELA AT THE AIRPORT CHECK-IN

Gabriela arrives at the airport. At the check-in counter, she says: "I would like a window seat, please." The agent asks: "May I see your passport?" Gabriela answers: "Of course. Could you check if my luggage is too heavy?" The agent says: "It is 22 kilos — that is fine. Would you like to check it in?" Gabriela says: "Yes, please. Could you also tell me which gate?" The agent replies: "Gate 14B. May I help you with anything else?"

1. What structure does Gabriela use to ask for a window seat politely?
A "Give me a window seat."
B "I would like a window seat, please."
C "I want window seat."
2. "May I see your passport?" is:
A A polite request for permission.
B A command.
C A past tense question.
3. What is the difference between "Could you" and "May I"?
A They mean exactly the same.
B "Could you" = asking someone to do something. "May I" = asking for permission.
C "Could you" is informal and "May I" is past tense.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip GRAMMAR

POLITE REQUESTS — MAY I / COULD YOU / I WOULD LIKE

No aeroporto e em situações formais, usamos estruturas educadas em vez de "I want" ou "Give me".

EstruturaUsoExemploTradução
May I + verbo?Pedir permissãoMay I see your passport?Posso ver seu passaporte?
Could you + verbo?Pedir algo a alguémCould you check my luggage?Você poderia verificar minha bagagem?
I would like + nounDizer o que quer (educado)I would like a window seat.Eu gostaria de um assento na janela.
Would you like + to?Oferecer algoWould you like to check it in?Você gostaria de despachar?

Dica: Nunca diga "I want" no aeroporto — use "I would like" ou "Could I have...?" para soar educado e natural.

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Lê a dica em inglês com calma — é assim que se aprende de verdade.

"Could I see your , please?"
Hint: the official document that proves your identity in international travel
"Here is my — I am in seat 24A."
Hint: the document that allows you to enter the plane
"Excuse me, where is B12?"
Hint: the place where you enter the plane
"I prefer a seat — I love to look outside."
Hint: the type of seat that lets you see the clouds
"My flight has a one-hour ."
Hint: when something happens later than expected
"This is my — I will not check it in."
Hint: the small bag you take with you on the plane

P2. Choose the best answer Quiz

Clica na resposta. Verde = certo, vermelho = tente de novo. Errou? Lê tudo de novo com calma.

You arrive at the airport and need to drop off your suitcase. You go to:
A "The gate."
B "The customs."
C "The check-in counter."
D "The boarding pass."
A flight attendant asks if you want a window seat or aisle seat. You like to look outside. You answer:
A "I would like an aisle seat, please."
B "I would like a window seat, please."
C "I would like a delay, please."
D "I would like a passport, please."

P3. Put this airport check-in dialogue in order Practice

Toque em cada item na ordem certa, ou use as setas para mover. Quando estiver pronta, clique em Check Order.

?"Yes, this one. And this is my carry-on bag."
?"Good morning! May I see your passport, please?"
?"Perfect. Here is your boarding pass. Your gate is B12."
?"Yes, here is my passport. I am flying to Paris."
?"Do you have any luggage to check in?"

P4. Read aloud Speaking

Ouça a frase, depois grave você falando. Use o Chrome — o sistema compara palavra por palavra.

May I see your boarding pass, please?
I would like a window seat, please.
Excuse me, where is gate B12?

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

Imagine you are at Guarulhos airport on the day of your trip to Paris. Tell the story of the next 4 hours in English — from arriving at the airport, checking in, going through security, to boarding the plane. Use the airport vocabulary as if you are narrating in real time.
Sugestão de resposta
“I am at the airport now. I go to the check-in counter and show my passport. The agent gives me my boarding pass. My gate is B12. I have one carry-on bag. After security, I walk to the gate. The flight attendant says it's time to board.”

Survival Card — Lesson 22: At the Airport

1May I see your passport, please?Posso ver seu passaporte, por favor?
2Where is gate B12, please?Onde fica o portão B12, por favor?
3I would like a window seat.Eu gostaria de um assento na janela.
4My flight has a one-hour delay.Meu voo tem uma hora de atraso.
5This is my carry-on luggage.Esta é minha bagagem de mão.
Aula 23 — Pre-class

On the Plane & Arriving in Paris — Flight Attendants, Customs & First Moments in France

Depois desta aula, você sobrevive ao voo, à imigração e ao primeiro contato real com Paris em inglês.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de responder a uma entrevista de imigração e a um voo inteiro em inglês, com 8+ frases naturais, sem precisar pedir tradução.

Vocabulário Vocabulário

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

turbulence·turbulência
“The turbulence was a little scary.”
tray table·mesa do avião
“Please put your tray table up.”
recline·reclinar
“Could I recline my seat?”
landing·pouso
“We are landing in 20 minutes.”
customs officer·agente da alfândega
“The customs officer asked many questions.”
immigration·imigração
“Immigration was very fast today.”
purpose of visit·motivo da viagem
“What is the purpose of your visit?”
length of stay·tempo de permanência
“What is your length of stay?”
I'm here as a tourist·estou aqui como turista
“I'm here as a tourist.”
accommodation·hospedagem
“My accommodation is a hotel in Paris.”

V1. Match the words to their meanings Vocabulary

Selecione a tradução correta para cada palavra. Errou? Leia o exemplo de novo — é aí que se aprende.

turbulence
tray table
recline
landing
customs officer
immigration
purpose of visit
accommodation

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas sobre a gramática.

GABRIELA'S FIRST FLIGHT — ARRIVING IN PARIS

The plane is landing in Paris. Gabriela is looking out the window. The flight attendant says: "Please fasten your seatbelt." Gabriela is feeling excited and nervous. The passengers are standing up. The captain says: "Please remain seated until the plane stops." At customs, the officer says: "Show me your passport, please." Gabriela answers: "Here it is. I am visiting Paris for the first time!"

1. What tense is "is landing" and "is looking"?
A Present Simple.
B Present Continuous (happening right now).
C Future with will.
2. "Please fasten your seatbelt" is:
A A question.
B An imperative (instruction/command).
C A past tense sentence.
3. How do we form the Present Continuous?
A Subject + verb + -ed
B Subject + am/is/are + verb-ing
C Subject + will + verb

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip GRAMMAR

PRESENT CONTINUOUS + IMPERATIVES

Usamos o Present Continuous para ações acontecendo AGORA. Usamos imperativos para instruções e pedidos.

EstruturaFórmulaExemploTradução
Present Continuous (+)S + am/is/are + V-ingI am waiting.Eu estou esperando.
Present Continuous (-)S + am/is/are + not + V-ingShe is not sleeping.Ela não está dormindo.
Present Continuous (?)Am/Is/Are + S + V-ing?Are you landing?Vocês estão pousando?
ImperativoVerbo (sem sujeito)Please fasten your seatbelt.Por favor, aperte o cinto.

Dica: O imperativo não usa sujeito — começa direto com o verbo: Show, Fasten, Please remain...

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Lê a dica em inglês com calma — é assim que se aprende de verdade.

"Please put your table up — we are landing soon."
Hint: the small table in front of your seat on the plane
"Could I my seat a little, please?"
Hint: to push your seat backwards
"We are in 20 minutes — please return to your seat."
Hint: when the plane touches the ground
"What is the of your visit, please?"
Hint: the reason WHY you are visiting a country
"I am here as a for ten days."
Hint: a person who visits a place for fun, not work
"My is a small hotel in Paris."
Hint: where you stay during your trip

P2. Choose the best answer Quiz

Clica na resposta. Verde = certo, vermelho = tente de novo. Errou? Lê tudo de novo com calma.

During the flight, you feel hot. The most polite way to ask is:
A "Bring me water!"
B "Could you bring me some water, please?"
C "Water now."
D "You bring water?"
A customs officer asks "What is the purpose of your visit?". The best answer is:
A "I have a passport."
B "My name is Gabriela."
C "I'm here as a tourist for ten days."
D "I do not know."

P3. Put this immigration interview in order Practice

Toque em cada item na ordem certa, ou use as setas para mover. Quando estiver pronta, clique em Check Order.

?"Of course. Here it is."
?"Welcome to France. Have a great trip!"
?"Bonjour. May I see your passport, please?"
?"I am staying at a small hotel in Paris."
?"Where is your accommodation?"
?"What is the purpose of your visit?"
?"I'm here as a tourist for ten days."

P4. Read aloud Speaking

Ouça a frase, depois grave você falando. Use o Chrome — o sistema compara palavra por palavra.

Could you bring me a blanket, please?
I'm here as a tourist for ten days.
My accommodation is a small hotel in Paris.

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

You just landed at Charles de Gaulle airport in Paris. The customs officer is asking you questions in English. Answer all of them — in full sentences, with confidence: name, nationality, purpose of visit, length of stay, where you are staying. This is the moment.
Sugestão de resposta
“Good morning. My name is Gabriela Pires. I am Brazilian. I am here as a tourist for ten days. I am staying at a small hotel in Montmartre. This is my first time in France and I am very excited.”

Survival Card — Lesson 23: On the Plane & Arriving in Paris

1Could you bring me a blanket, please?Você poderia me trazer um cobertor, por favor?
2I'm here as a tourist for ten days.Estou aqui como turista por dez dias.
3My accommodation is a hotel in Paris.Minha hospedagem é um hotel em Paris.
4Could I recline my seat, please?Posso reclinar meu assento, por favor?
5We are landing in 20 minutes.Vamos pousar em 20 minutos.
Aula 24 — Pre-class

Milestone Check-In — Halfway Point: What She Knows, What She Can Do & Where She's Going

Depois desta aula, você comprova: comparando hoje com a Aula 1, sabe quem virou — e o que ainda falta.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de descrever seu próprio progresso comparando Aula 1 com Aula 24, identificando 3 ganhos concretos e 2 pontos de atenção em inglês.

Vocabulário Vocabulário

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

I can·eu consigo
“Now I can introduce myself in English.”
I couldn't·eu não conseguia
“Before, I couldn't order in English.”
I've improved in·eu melhorei em
“I've improved in pronunciation.”
I'm still working on·ainda estou trabalhando em
“I'm still working on listening.”
confident·confiante
“I feel confident with greetings.”
progress·progresso
“I am proud of my progress.”
goal·meta
“My goal is to be fluent by 2027.”
looking back·olhando para trás
“Looking back, the first class felt scary.”
achievement·conquista
“This is a big achievement for me.”
milestone·marco
“This class is an important milestone.”

V1. Match the words to their meanings Vocabulary

Selecione a tradução correta para cada palavra. Errou? Leia o exemplo de novo — é aí que se aprende.

I can
I couldn't
I've improved in
confident
progress
goal
achievement
milestone

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas sobre a gramática.

GABRIELA'S MILESTONE — LOOKING BACK AND FORWARD

In Aula 1, I couldn't say my name in English. Now I can introduce myself with confidence. Before, I couldn't understand simple questions. Now I can answer them without translating from Portuguese. I can talk about my daily routine, my favorites, and my family. I still can't understand fast English on TV, but I can follow my teacher. My goal is: by Aula 48, I will be able to have a full conversation in English.

1. What could Gabriela NOT do in Aula 1?
A She couldn't say her name in English.
B She couldn't watch TV.
C She couldn't go to school.
2. What is the difference between "can" and "could" in this text?
A Can = past, could = present.
B Can = present ability, couldn't = past inability.
C They mean the same thing.
3. What does "will be able to" express?
A A past ability.
B A present routine.
C A future ability.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip GRAMMAR

CAN / CAN'T / COULD / COULDN'T — HABILIDADE

Usamos can para dizer o que conseguimos fazer agora e could para o que conseguíamos (ou não) no passado.

TempoAfirmativoNegativoTradução
PresenteI can speak English.I can't speak fast.Eu consigo / Eu não consigo
PassadoI could swim at 5.I couldn't say my name.Eu conseguia / Eu não conseguia
FuturoI will be able to travel.—Eu vou conseguir

Pergunta: Can you speak English? / Could you swim when you were 5?

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Lê a dica em inglês com calma — é assim que se aprende de verdade.

"In Aula 1, I even say my name in English."
Hint: the past form of "cannot" — it shows ability you did not have
"Now I introduce myself with confidence."
Hint: the modal verb that shows present ability
"I am very when I speak about my routine."
Hint: an adjective that means feeling sure of yourself
"I am proud of my in pronunciation."
Hint: the noun that means moving forward and getting better
"My biggest is speaking in English without fear."
Hint: something difficult that you accomplished
"My next is to handle a full conversation in Paris."
Hint: a future target you want to reach

P2. Choose the best answer Quiz

Clica na resposta. Verde = certo, vermelho = tente de novo. Errou? Lê tudo de novo com calma.

Looking back from Aula 1 to Aula 24, the most accurate self-statement is:
A "I am the same as Aula 1."
B "In Aula 1, I couldn't introduce myself. Now I can."
C "I will speak Portuguese."
D "I have not changed."
You want to celebrate progress without arrogance. The best phrase is:
A "I am perfect now."
B "I am proud of my progress and I am still working on listening."
C "I have nothing left to learn."
D "I do not need more classes."

P3. Put this halfway-point self-reflection in order Practice

Toque em cada item na ordem certa, ou use as setas para mover. Quando estiver pronta, clique em Check Order.

?"Now I can introduce myself with confidence."
?"I am still working on listening to fast English."
?"Looking back at Aula 1, I was very nervous."
?"My next goal is to survive Paris in English in 2027."
?"At first, I couldn't even say my name in English."

P4. Read aloud Speaking

Ouça a frase, depois grave você falando. Use o Chrome — o sistema compara palavra por palavra.

In Aula 1, I couldn't even introduce myself. Now I can.
I'm proud of my progress and I'm still working on listening.
My next goal is to survive Paris in English in 2027.

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

It's the halfway point of your program — Aula 24 of 48. Imagine your Aula 1 self is in the room with you. Tell her three things she WILL achieve, three things she will improve at, and one thing she should be proud of starting today. Speak to her in English.
Sugestão de resposta
“Hi, Aula 1 Gabriela. You will introduce yourself in English by Aula 5. You will tell stories in English by Aula 14. You will land in Paris in 2027 ready to talk. You will improve at listening, at speaking without translating, and at making jokes in English. Be proud — you started, and that is the hardest part.”

Survival Card — Lesson 24: Milestone Check-In

1In Aula 1, I couldn't introduce myself.Na Aula 1, eu não conseguia me apresentar.
2Now I can speak English with confidence.Agora eu consigo falar inglês com confiança.
3I'm proud of my progress.Estou orgulhosa do meu progresso.
4I'm still working on listening.Ainda estou trabalhando na escuta.
5My next goal is Paris 2027.Minha próxima meta é Paris 2027.
Aula 25 — Pre-class

Let's Talk About Series! — Describing TV Shows, Giving Opinions & Using Pop Culture as Grammar Tools

Depois desta aula, você defende sua série favorita com argumento, opinião e personagens descritos como crítica de TV.
0%
▼

Objetivo: Ao final desta aula, Gabriela será capaz de descrever uma série favorita usando 4+ relative clauses com who/that, dar opinião com 3+ marcadores ("I think", "in my opinion") e recomendar a alguém em 4+ frases.

Vocabulário Vocabulário

Olha a palavra, a tradução e o exemplo juntos. Toca em Ouvir — o som ajuda muito a memória.

episode·episódio
“The first episode hooked me immediately.”
season·temporada
“Season two is much better than season one.”
plot·enredo
“The plot is full of surprises.”
character·personagem
“Blair is the character who never gives up.”
main character·protagonista
“Serena is the main character of Gossip Girl.”
villain·vilão
“Chuck is sometimes the villain.”
binge-watch·maratonar
“I love to binge-watch on weekends.”
cliffhanger·gancho
“Season finales always end with a cliffhanger.”
I think·eu acho
“I think Blair is the best character.”
in my opinion·na minha opinião
“In my opinion, Friends is funnier.”

V1. Match the words to their meanings Vocabulary

Selecione a tradução correta para cada palavra. Errou? Leia o exemplo de novo — é aí que se aprende.

episode
season
plot
main character
villain
binge-watch
cliffhanger
in my opinion

Stage 1.2: Grammar in Context GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas sobre a gramática.

GABRIELA TALKS ABOUT HER FAVORITE SERIES

My favorite series is Gossip Girl because it is very exciting. The characters are interesting and the story is never boring. I think it is the best show on TV. I also like Friends because it is really funny, so I watch it when I am sad. I don't like horror series because they are too scary. In my opinion, a good series needs good acting and a surprising story.

1. Why does Gabriela like Gossip Girl?
A Because it is boring.
B Because it is exciting.
C Because it is scary.
2. What word connects a reason in "I watch Friends because it is funny"?
A so
B because
C and
3. What does "so" mean in "it is funny, so I watch it"?
A It gives the reason.
B It gives the result.
C It gives an example.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip GRAMMAR

OPINION ADJECTIVES + BECAUSE / SO

Usamos adjetivos de opinião para descrever séries, filmes e programas. Conectamos com because (razão) e so (resultado).

AdjectiveTraduçãoExample
excitingemocionanteIt is exciting.
boringentedianteIt is boring.
funnyengraçadoIt is funny.
scaryassustadorIt is scary.
surprisingsurpreendenteIt is surprising.

Because (razão): I like it because it is funny. (Por que gosta? Porque é engraçado.)

So (resultado): It is funny, so I watch it every day. (É engraçado, então assisto todo dia.)

P1. Complete with the correct word Practice

Faça um de cada vez. Errou? Lê a dica em inglês com calma — é assim que se aprende de verdade.

"My favorite of Friends is the one in the embassy."
Hint: one part of a series — about 22 minutes for Friends
" two of Gossip Girl is much better than season one."
Hint: a group of episodes released together (10–22 episodes)
"The of Notting Hill is so romantic."
Hint: the main story of a movie or TV show
"Blair is the who has the best style."
Hint: a person inside a story
"On weekends, I love to -watch series in bed."
Hint: to watch many episodes one after another
"In my , Rory is the most relatable character."
Hint: what you personally think about something

P2. Choose the best answer Quiz

Clica na resposta. Verde = certo, vermelho = tente de novo. Errou? Lê tudo de novo com calma.

You describe Blair Waldorf to a French friend. The best sentence using a relative clause is:
A "Blair is the smart girl."
B "Blair is a character who is ambitious and stylish."
C "Blair: ambitious. Stylish."
D "Blair, ambitious girl, stylish."
You want to give an opinion about Friends without sounding aggressive. The best opener is:
A "Friends is the best, period."
B "In my opinion, Friends is funnier than Gossip Girl."
C "You are wrong about Friends."
D "Friends — you understand nothing."

P3. Put this Gossip Girl show recommendation in order Practice

Toque em cada item na ordem certa, ou use as setas para mover. Quando estiver pronta, clique em Check Order.

?"In my opinion, you should definitely binge-watch the first season!"
?"Blair is the character who has the best style and is so dramatic."
?"The main character is Serena, who is rich and famous."
?"It's a show that takes place in New York."
?"Have you ever watched Gossip Girl?"

P4. Read aloud Speaking

Ouça a frase, depois grave você falando. Use o Chrome — o sistema compara palavra por palavra.

Blair is the character who has the best style.
Friends is a show that takes place in New York.
In my opinion, you should definitely binge-watch it.

T1. Think and respond Reflection

Aqui não tem resposta certa — é pra você pensar em inglês. Toque no microfone e responde como vier.

A French friend in Paris asks "What show should I watch this weekend?" Recommend one of YOUR favorite series in English — describe the plot, the main characters using relative clauses (who/that), and your opinion. Make her want to watch it.
Sugestão de resposta
“You should watch Gossip Girl — it's a show that takes place in New York. The main character is Serena, who is glamorous but kind. Blair is the character who has the best style and is so dramatic. In my opinion, the first season is the best — definitely binge-watch it.”

Survival Card — Lesson 25: Let's Talk About Series!

1Friends is a show that takes place in New York.Friends é uma série que se passa em Nova York.
2Blair is the character who has the best style.Blair é a personagem que tem o melhor estilo.
3In my opinion, season two is the best.Na minha opinião, a segunda temporada é a melhor.
4You should definitely binge-watch it.Você deveria com certeza maratonar.
5The plot is full of surprises.O enredo é cheio de surpresas.

Atividades Complementares

Séries, filmes e canais para acelerar seu progresso entre as aulas.

Series Teen — Conectadas com as Aulas

Series escolhidas para reforcar o vocabulario de cada bloco de aulas. Assista com legendas em ingles.

Serie (Aulas 1-2)
Gossip Girl — Season 1, Episode 1: "Pilot"

Nos primeiros 10 minutos, Serena volta para Manhattan e se apresenta: "My name is Serena", "I live in New York". Blair pergunta "How are you?". Usa exatamente o vocabulario das Aulas 1 e 2: name, live, hello, nice to meet you.

Dica: Anote 3 cumprimentos que ouvir (hello, hi, how are you). Compare com as frases da Aula 2: "Hello! How are you?"
Serie (Aula 3)
Friends — Season 1, Episode 1: "The One Where Monica Gets a Roommate"

Rachel chega e os amigos usam "this is" e "that is" para apresentar objetos e pessoas: "This is my apartment", "That is Monica's room". Conecta com demonstrativos this/that/these/those da Aula 3.

Dica: Conte quantas vezes ouve "this is" ou "that is" nos primeiros 10 minutos. Anote pelo menos 3.
Serie (Aula 4)
Gilmore Girls — Season 1, Episode 1: "Pilot" (cena do cafe da manha)

Rory e Lorelai descrevem a rotina matinal: "I wake up at six", "I always have coffee first", "I usually walk to school". Usa os verbos de rotina e adverbios de frequencia da Aula 4.

Dica: Anote 3 verbos de rotina em ingles que voce ouviu (wake up, have breakfast, walk). Compare com a sua rotina em Sao Paulo.
Serie (Aula 5)
Gossip Girl — Season 1, Episode 3: "Poison Ivy"

Blair e Serena falam do que gostam e nao gostam: "I like shopping", "I don't like waiting". Conecta com like/don't like da Aula 5: "Do you like it?"

Dica: Anote 3 frases com "like" ou "don't like". Depois escreva 3 sobre voce: "I like watching Gossip Girl", "I don't like waking up early".

Paris & Viagem 2027 — Conectadas com Aulas 6-8

Vocabulario das aulas: numbers, time, dates, countries, nationalities, can, abilities.

YouTube (Aula 6)
Emily in Paris — Season 1, Episode 1: "Emily in Paris" (primeiros 15 min)

Emily chega em Paris e lida com numeros, horarios e datas: "My flight is at 3pm", "The meeting is on Monday". Usa exatamente o vocabulario da Aula 6: numbers, time, dates.

Dica: Anote 3 momentos onde Emily fala horarios ou datas. Pratique: "My class is at [hora]", "My birthday is on [data]".
Watch on YouTube ↗
YouTube (Aula 7)
Prowalk Tours — "Walking Paris: Eiffel Tower to Champs-Elysees" (narrado em ingles)

O narrador fala de paises e nacionalidades dos turistas: "tourists from Japan", "a Brazilian couple", "French architecture". Conecta com countries e nationalities da Aula 7.

Dica: Anote todas as nacionalidades que ouvir. Depois pratique: "I am from Brazil. I am Brazilian. I want to travel to France."
Watch on YouTube ↗
YouTube (Aula 8)
Lost LeBlanc — "First Time in Paris: What You Need to Know" (guia pratico)

LeBlanc mostra situacoes reais em Paris: "Can you help me?", "Can I take a photo?", "I can speak a little French". Usa "can" para habilidades e pedidos, exatamente o tema da Aula 8.

Dica: Anote 5 frases com "can". Pratique para sua viagem: "Can you take a photo?", "I can speak English", "Can I have the menu?".
Watch on YouTube ↗

Pronuncia & Vocabulario Diario

Recursos para pratica diaria de pronuncia e vocabulario, conectados ao nivel A1 da Gabriela.

YouTube
English with Lucy — "50 Common English Phrases for Beginners" (8 min)

Lucy ensina 50 frases essenciais para iniciantes, incluindo muitas da Aula 1 e 2: "My name is...", "I live in...", "Nice to meet you", "How are you?". Pronuncia clara e ritmo lento.

Dica: Assista e repita cada frase. Marque as que voce ja aprendeu nas aulas.
Watch on YouTube ↗
App
Duolingo Stories — "The Introduction" + "At the Cafe"

Duas historias especificas: "The Introduction" reforça vocabulario da Aula 1 (name, live, student) e "At the Cafe" pratica numeros e pedidos da Aula 6. Suporte em PT-BR para iniciantes.

Dica: 15 min por dia. Comece por "The Introduction" e depois faca "At the Cafe" quando chegar na Aula 6.
YouTube
Easy English — Episode: "People in London Introduce Themselves" (com legendas)

Pessoas reais na rua se apresentam: "My name is...", "I'm from...", "I live in...". Ingles real com legendas, conectando com Aulas 1 e 7 (apresentacao + paises/nacionalidades).

Dica: Assista e tente entender as apresentacoes. Anote: de que pais sao? Onde moram? Vocabulario identico as aulas.
Watch on YouTube ↗
Podcast
6 Minute English (BBC) — Episode: "Learning a new language"

Episodio sobre aprender idiomas novos: "I want to learn English", "My favorite way to learn is...". Usa vocabulario das Aulas 1 e 5: learn, favorite, like. Ritmo lento, 6 minutos.

Dica: Ouva no caminho da escola. Anote 1 expressao nova. Compare: "I want to learn English" = exatamente a frase da Aula 1!
Listen on Spotify ↗

Por Aula — Conexoes Diretas com o Vocabulario

Um recurso especifico por aula, conectado ao vocabulario e tema daquela aula.

Aula 1: Who Is Gabriela?
Friends — S01E01: "The One Where Monica Gets a Roommate" (primeiros 5 min)

Os amigos se apresentam para Rachel: "My name is...", "I live here", "I'm a student". Vocabulario identico a Aula 1 (name, live, student, hobby, dream). Anote 3 frases que entendeu.

Aula 2: Hello, Nice to Meet You!
Rachel's English — "American English Alphabet Pronunciation" (5 min)

Pronuncia de cada letra do alfabeto americano. Conecta com "spell" e "last name" da Aula 2: "Can you spell your name? P-I-R-E-S." Anote as 3 letras mais diferentes do portugues.

Aula 3: This Is My World
Gossip Girl — Season 1, Episode 2: "The Wild Brunch" (qualquer cena de 5 min)

Conte quantas vezes ouve "this", "that", "these" ou "those" em uma cena. Conecta com os demonstrativos da Aula 3: "This is my phone", "That is my school".

Aula 4: My Daily Life
Gilmore Girls — Season 1, Episode 1: "Pilot" (cena do cafe da manha, 8 min)

Rory descreve a rotina: "I wake up early", "I usually have coffee", "I go to school at 7". Anote 3 verbos de rotina que ouviu. Conecta com Present Simple da Aula 4.

Aula 5
Friends — 10 min com legenda EN

Conte quantas vezes ouve "I like", "I love" ou "I don't like". Escreva 3 frases.

Aula 9: Where Is It?
Emily in Paris — Season 1, Episode 3: "Sexy or Sexist" (cena da rua, 8 min)

Emily esta perdida em Paris e pede direcoes: "Excuse me, where is the metro?", "Go straight", "Turn left at the corner". Usa exatamente o vocabulario da Aula 9: street, corner, straight, turn, cross. Anote 3 direcoes que voce ouvir.

Aula 9: Where Is It?
6 Minute English (BBC) — Episode: "Getting around a new city"

Como se orientar numa cidade nova: "Where is the station?", "It is near the park", "Go straight and turn right". Usa preposicoes de lugar e direcoes, exatamente o tema da Aula 9. Ritmo lento, 6 minutos.

Dica: Ouca e anote as preposicoes: next to, in front of, across from, behind. Pratique: "The cafe is next to the bank."
Listen on Spotify ↗
Aula 9: Where Is It?
English with Lucy — "Giving Directions in English" (7 min)

Lucy ensina como dar e pedir direcoes: "Go straight", "Turn left", "It is on the corner", "Cross the street". Pronuncia clara e exemplos visuais com mapa. Conecta com todo o vocabulario da Aula 9.

Dica: Pause e repita cada direcao. Depois descreva como chegar da sua casa ate a escola usando: "Go straight, turn right, it is next to..."
Watch on YouTube ↗
Aula 10: What Do You Look Like?
Gossip Girl — Season 1, Episode 4: "Bad News Blair" (primeiros 10 min)

Blair descreve as modelos no photoshoot: "She is tall", "She has long blond hair", "He has blue eyes and a beard". Usa exatamente o vocabulario da Aula 10: tall, short, hair, eyes, curly, blond. Anote 3 descricoes fisicas que ouvir.

Aula 10: What Do You Look Like?
6 Minute English (BBC) — Episode: "Describing people"

Como descrever pessoas em ingles: "She is tall with curly hair", "He has glasses and a beard", "She has brown eyes". Vocabulario identico a Aula 10. Ritmo lento com explicacoes simples.

Dica: Ouca e descreva 3 pessoas da sua familia usando: "My mom is tall. She has long dark hair. She has brown eyes."
Listen on Spotify ↗
Aula 10: What Do You Look Like?
English with Lucy — "How to Describe People's Appearance" (9 min)

Lucy ensina adjetivos de aparencia fisica: tall, short, curly, straight, blond, glasses, beard. Mostra fotos e pratica: "She has long blond hair and blue eyes", "He is short and he has a beard". Conecta com todo o vocabulario da Aula 10.

Dica: Pause e descreva seu personagem favorito de Gossip Girl: "Serena is tall. She has long blond hair. She has blue eyes."
Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 11 -- Let's Eat!

Series
Emily in Paris -- restaurant and cafe scenes (Season 1)

Watch the cafe and restaurant scenes. Notice how people read the menu, talk to the waiter, and say "I would like..." to order food and a drink. Listen for dessert and bill at the end.

Tip: Write down five food words you hear and one sentence with "I would like".

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Ordering Food at a Restaurant"

A short, clear episode about ordering food in English: greeting the waiter, choosing a dish, asking for a drink and dessert, and asking for the bill at the end.

Tip: Record yourself ordering a full meal: a drink, a main dish, and a dessert. Use "I would like".

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "some, any and countable / uncountable nouns"

A focused lesson on countable and uncountable food words and how to use some and any. It reinforces today's grammar for talking about food and drinks at a restaurant.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences about a meal: three with some and three with any.

Lesson 12 -- Shopping in the City

Series
Emily in Paris -- shopping and boutique scenes (Season 1)

Watch the shopping scenes in Paris. Notice how people ask "How much is it?", talk about the size and price, and compare two items with cheaper and more expensive before they choose.

Tip: Write down five shop words you hear and one comparative sentence (for example, with cheaper or more expensive).

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Shopping for Clothes"

A short, clear episode about shopping for clothes in English: asking the price, talking about sizes, trying clothes on in the fitting room, and paying at the end.

Tip: Record yourself comparing two items: one cheaper and one more expensive. Then choose one and say why.

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "Comparative adjectives"

A focused lesson on comparative adjectives: how to add -er to short words (cheaper, bigger) and use more with long words (more expensive). It reinforces today's grammar for comparing clothes and prices.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences comparing clothes: three with -er and three with more.

Lesson 13 -- Getting Around the City

Series
Emily in Paris -- moving around the city scenes (Season 1)

Watch the scenes where Emily takes the metro and walks around Paris. Notice how people ask for directions with "Where is...?" and "How do I get to...?", and how they give directions with go straight and turn left.

Tip: Write down five city words you hear and one direction sentence with an imperative (for example, with go straight or turn left).

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Asking for and Giving Directions"

A short, clear episode about getting around a city in English: asking the way, taking the subway and the bus, and following directions to find a place.

Tip: Record yourself giving directions from your home to your school: go straight, turn left or right, and get off at a stop.

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "Asking for directions"

A focused lesson on directions: how to ask "Where is...?" and "How do I get to...?", and how to give directions with the imperative (go straight, turn left, turn right). It reinforces today's grammar for finding your way.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences: three questions to ask the way, and three directions with go straight, turn left or get off.

Lesson 14 -- Eating Out Like a Local

Series
Emily in Paris -- restaurant and dinner scenes (Season 1)

Watch the scenes where the characters eat out in Paris. Notice how people order food with "I would like" and "Could I have", and how they ask the waiter to recommend a dish.

Tip: Write down five restaurant words you hear and one polite order with "I would like" or "Could I have".

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Ordering Food at a Restaurant"

A short, clear episode about eating out in English: booking a table, asking the waiter to recommend a dish, ordering an appetizer and a main course, and paying the check.

Tip: Record yourself ordering a full dinner: an appetizer, a main course, and ask for the check at the end.

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "At a restaurant"

A focused lesson on eating out: how to order politely with "I would like" and "Could I have", and how to ask the waiter to recommend something. It reinforces today's polite ordering language.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences: three polite orders with "I would like", and three requests with "Could I have" or "Can you recommend".

Lesson 15 -- A Day at the Museum

Series
Night at the Museum -- the museum tour scenes

Watch the scenes inside the museum. Notice the words for the galleries, paintings and sculptures, and how the characters describe what they see with "There is" and "There are".

Tip: Write down five museum words you hear and one sentence with "There is" and one with "There are".

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Visiting a Museum"

A short, clear episode about a day at a museum in English: buying a ticket, asking how much it costs, finding the entrance, and talking about the exhibition and the art inside.

Tip: Record yourself buying a ticket and describing a gallery: ask "How much is", then use "There is" and "There are".

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "At the museum"

A focused lesson on visiting a museum: how to ask prices with "How much is" and "How much are", and how to describe an exhibition with "There is" and "There are". It reinforces today's grammar.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences: two questions with "How much is/are", and four descriptions with "There is/are".

Lesson 16 -- Asking About the City

Series
Emily in Paris -- the sightseeing scenes

Watch the scenes where Emily explores Paris and visits the landmarks. Notice how people ask for information and talk about the tour, the view, and the places nearby.

Tip: Write down five tour words you hear and one question that starts with "Can you tell me".

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Asking for Information"

A short, clear episode about asking for information politely in English: how to start with "Can you tell me", how to ask if something exists with "Is there", and how to talk about a tour.

Tip: Record yourself asking three polite questions on a city tour: use "Can you tell me" twice and "Is there" once.

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "Asking for information"

A focused lesson on getting information politely: how to use "Can you tell me..." with where, what time and how long, and how to ask "Is there...?" It reinforces today's grammar.

Tip: After watching, write six questions: four with "Can you tell me" and two with "Is there".

Lesson 17 -- Buying Gifts & Making Choices

Series
Gilmore Girls -- the gift shopping scenes

Watch the scenes where the characters buy presents in a store. Notice how they choose between things with "one" and "ones" and ask "Which one?".

Tip: Write down five gift words you hear and one sentence with "I'll take the ... one".

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Shopping for Gifts"

A short, clear episode about buying gifts in English: how to choose with "one" and "ones", how to ask "Which one?", and how to say "I'll take..." in a shop.

Tip: Record yourself buying three gifts: use "one" once, "ones" once, and "I'll take" once.

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "one and ones"

A focused lesson on using "one" and "ones" to avoid repeating the noun, plus "Which one?" for making choices. It reinforces today's grammar.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences: three with "one" and three with "ones".

Lesson 18 -- Sending a Postcard

Series
Emily in Paris -- the letter and message scenes

Watch a scene where a character writes a message or sends mail. Notice how people use short words like "it", "them", "her" and "him" instead of repeating the name.

Tip: Write down three sentences you hear with "it" or "them", and say who each one is about.

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "At the Post Office"

A short, clear episode about sending mail in English: how to ask for a stamp, talk about sending things abroad by air mail, and use "send it to her / him".

Tip: Record yourself sending three things: use "it" once, "them" once, and "her" or "him" once.

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "object pronouns: it, them, her, him"

A focused lesson on object pronouns: when to use "it" for one thing, "them" for many, "her" for a woman and "him" for a man. It reinforces today's grammar.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences about sending mail, one for each: it, them, her, him.

Lesson 19 -- Making Plans with Friends

Series
Heartstopper -- the friends making weekend plans

Watch the scenes where the teenagers arrange to meet up and hang out. Notice how they say "Are you free?", "Let's meet up" and "I'm meeting...".

Tip: Write down three sentences they use to make a plan, with a time and a place.

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Making Plans with Friends"

A short, clear episode about arranging to meet up in English: how to ask "Are you free?", how to suggest with "Let's" and "Why don't we", and how to talk about a plan with "I'm meeting...".

Tip: Record yourself making a plan: use "I'm meeting" once, "Let's" once, and "on time" once.

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "Present continuous for future plans"

A focused lesson on using "I'm meeting" and "We're going" to talk about arranged future plans, plus "Let's" and "Why don't we" for suggestions. It reinforces today's grammar.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences about your weekend plans with "be + verb-ing".

Lesson 20 -- My Paris Trip So Far

Series
Emily in Paris -- the travel moments

Watch the scenes where characters talk about what they have done and seen in Paris. Notice the present perfect: "I have visited...", "Have you ever...?".

Tip: Write down three sentences you hear with "have" + verb, then say one about your own week.

Podcast
Culips ESL -- "Talking About Experiences"

A short, clear episode about the present perfect: how to use "already", "yet", "just" and "ever" to talk about what you have done in your life and on a trip.

Tip: Record yourself saying three things you have already done today and one thing you have not done yet.

YouTube
BBC Learning English -- "Present Perfect: already, yet, just"

A focused lesson on the present perfect with "already", "yet" and "just", plus "ever" for life experiences. It reinforces today's grammar.

Tip: After watching, write six sentences about your trip or week: two with "already", two with "yet", two with "just".
